name: ai-prompt-chip
title: AI Prompt Chip
description: Prompt starter whose insert-or-submit behaviour is explicit in the icon and the accessible name.

props.name: prompt
props.description: The prompt text handed back to the caller.

props.name: mode
props.description: Required. `insert` fills the composer for editing; `submit` sends immediately.

props.name: onSelect
props.description: Receives the prompt and the mode so one handler can serve both kinds of chip.

clinicalNotes: `mode` is required on purpose. A chip that silently sends a prompt about the open patient — while the clinician expected to review the wording first — is a consent and accuracy problem, not a UX nit. The mode is therefore carried three ways: the icon (plus vs return arrow), the accessible name ("Insert prompt into composer" vs "Send prompt"), and a data-mode attribute for tests. Prefer `insert` for anything that will carry patient context into a request.
notes: Renders a Button, so it inherits focus-visible rings, disabled handling, and keyboard activation for free.
